skill.name: gwas-database
skill.description: Query the NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog for SNP-trait associations, variant details, trait searches, gene lookups, p-values, effect sizes, and summary statistics.
skill.summary: Find and interpret GWAS Catalog associations for variants, traits, genes, studies, and summary statistics.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown documentation and biomedical identifier false positives rather than executable malware. The skill does guide users to public NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog APIs, FTP or HTTP download locations, and an optional local file write example, so publication is appropriate with low-risk network and filesystem disclosure.

low_findings.title: Public GWAS API Network Access
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples that call NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog REST APIs and summary statistics endpoints. This is expected for the skill purpose and uses public scientific data endpoints, but users should understand that queries leave the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly document EBI API URLs and Python requests calls. The context is legitimate GWAS data retrieval, not covert exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Optional Summary Statistics Download Writes Files
low_findings.description: The API reference includes a helper example that streams a public summary statistics file to an output path. The write is user-directed and limited to downloaded GWAS data, but it is still a real filesystem operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code opens an output file in binary write mode and writes response chunks. No evidence shows hidden paths, credential files, or unauthorized file access.

low_findings.title: FTP and Command Examples Require User Intent
low_findings.description: The skill references FTP download locations and a wget example for public GWAS summary statistics. This is not malicious, but users should prefer trusted sources and avoid running generated commands without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and FTP URLs are visible documentation examples for public EBI data. The skill contains no script that runs them automatically.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby or shell backtick detections correspond to Markdown inline code, fenced code blocks, endpoint names, and data field names. No executable Ruby code or automatic shell execution was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are Markdown syntax and Python documentation examples, not Ruby backtick execution. The repository contains only SKILL.md and references/api_reference.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Biomedical Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match biomedical terms, identifiers, or Markdown text such as rs ID, PMID, and API descriptions. No hashing, encryption, or credential-handling code was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations describe GWAS APIs and publication identifiers, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or cryptographic operations. No crypto API usage was present in context.

content.value_statement: Genetic association research requires accurate variant, trait, and study lookups across specialized databases. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through GWAS Catalog searches, API use, and result interpretation.
actual_capabilities: Find GWAS Catalog associations for rs IDs, disease traits, genes, chromosomal regions, and publications.
Explain REST API endpoints for studies, associations, variants, traits, genes, and publications.
Guide retrieval of p-values, effect sizes, risk alleles, EFO terms, PubMed IDs, and study metadata.
Support summary statistics workflows using the GWAS summary statistics API and public download locations.
Recommend interpretation checks for ancestry, sample size, replication, p-value thresholds, and data quality.
Provide Python-oriented query patterns for pagination, rate limiting, error handling, and tabular analysis.
limitations: It depends on live GWAS Catalog availability and current API behavior.
It does not validate clinical actionability or provide medical diagnosis.
It cannot guarantee that every GWAS publication or summary statistic file is available.
Large summary statistics downloads may require storage planning and user review before execution.

use_cases.title: Variant Association Review
use_cases.description: Look up a candidate rs ID and summarize associated traits, p-values, effect alleles, study accessions, and publication context.

use_cases.title: Trait Evidence Collection
use_cases.description: Collect genome-wide significant associations for a disease or phenotype and prepare a reproducible evidence table.

use_cases.title: Study and Summary Statistics Triage
use_cases.description: Identify available GWAS studies, check metadata, and decide whether summary statistics downloads are needed.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the GWAS Catalog skill to look up rs7903146. Summarize associated traits, strongest evidence, p-values, and study references.

prompt_templates.title: Find Trait Associations
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the GWAS Catalog for type 2 diabetes associations. Focus on genome-wide significant variants, EFO terms, effect sizes, and ancestry notes.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Reproducible Query Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reproducible GWAS Catalog API workflow for the APOE gene. Include endpoints, parameters, pagination, fields to extract, and quality checks.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Summary Statistics Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether GWAS summary statistics are needed for GCST study accessions I provide. Compare API queries, FTP downloads, storage needs, and citation requirements.

output_examples.output: A concise evidence summary listing associated traits, strongest p-values, reported alleles, study accessions, PubMed references, and cautions about ancestry and replication.

output_examples.input: Plan a type 2 diabetes GWAS Catalog search.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step query plan covering EFO trait selection, association endpoint use, pagination, genome-wide significance filtering, metadata review, and citation notes.

output_examples.input: Should I download summary statistics for this study?
output_examples.output: A decision checklist covering availability, file size, harmonized data needs, local storage, rate limits, and whether targeted API queries are enough.

best_practices: Confirm EFO terms and study accessions before running broad queries.
Record query dates, endpoints, parameters, and GWAS Catalog citations for reproducibility.
Review ancestry, sample size, replication, and effect-size harmonization before drawing conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat GWAS associations as clinical recommendations without expert validation.
Do not run large downloads or generated shell commands without reviewing paths and storage impact.
Do not combine effect sizes across studies without checking overlap, ancestry, and allele harmonization.

faq.question: Does this skill require API keys?
faq.answer: No. The documented NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog APIs are public and do not require authentication.

faq.question: Can it search by rs ID?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides workflows for variant lookup and association retrieval using rs identifiers.

faq.question: Can it retrieve full summary statistics?
faq.answer: It can guide access to available summary statistics through public APIs or download locations. Availability varies by study.

faq.question: Is this a medical diagnosis tool?
faq.answer: No. It supports research interpretation of published GWAS data and should not be used for diagnosis or treatment decisions.

faq.question: What tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing results?
faq.answer: Users should verify p-value thresholds, ancestry context, sample size, replication evidence, original studies, and GWAS Catalog citation requirements.
